methods.description: Process for determining depth-specific anaerobic oxidation of methane (AOM) rates in Lake Sempach sediments using a subcore-incubation approach.

steps.description: Subcores were collected by inserting three small plexiglass tubes (inner diameter 16 mm) into the fresh sediment core, and both ends of the core tubes were sealed with black rubber stoppers.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment subcores using plexiglass tubes

steps.description: After ~1 h preincubation, 20 μl of 14 CH4 solution (~2.5 kBq) was injected through each of the predrilled, silicone-sealed ports at 1.5 cm intervals.
Subcores were incubated at in situ temperature (4°C) in the dark for 2 days.
steps.searchable_summary: incubate subcores at 4°C in the dark

steps.description: At the end of the incubation, subcores were extruded, and subsamples were collected at 1 or 2 cm resolution, and directly transferred into 100 ml vials containing 20 ml of the aqueous NaOH (5% w:w) to terminate microbial activity.
steps.searchable_summary: extrude subcores and terminate microbial activity with NaOH

steps.description: Radioactivity in different 14C pools in the incubation samples and AOM rates were determined according to Su et al. (2019).

methods.description: Procedure for extracting and analyzing microbial lipids from sediment samples after incubation with 13CH4.

steps.description: Triplicate slurries were combined and freeze-dried.
Samples were homogenized for lipid extraction using an Accelerated Solvent Extraction system.
Samples were extracted with a solvent mixture of dichloromethane and methanol (9:1, vol/vol) over three extraction cycles at 100°C and maximum pressure of 1,500 psi.
Total lipid extracts (TLEs) were collected in 60 ml glass vials, and concentrated using a vacuum evaporator system.
The TLEs were further evaporated to dryness and then saponified with methanolic KOH-solution (12%) at 80°C for 3 h.
Neutral compounds were extracted by liquid-liquid extraction using hexane.
Fatty acids (FAs) were extracted from the remaining aqueous phase with hexane after acidification (pH ~1).
steps.searchable_summary: extract fatty acids with hexane after acidification

steps.description: The fatty acid fraction was methylated using BF3 in methanol (14% v/v) at 80°C for 2 h.
steps.searchable_summary: methylate fatty acids with BF3 in methanol

steps.description: Neutral compounds were further separated into hydrocarbon and alcohol fractions over silica glass gel columns.
steps.searchable_summary: separate neutral compounds using silica gel columns

steps.description: The alcohol fraction was methylated with 100 μl pyridine and 50 μl bis(trimethylsilyl)trifluoracetamide (BSTFA) at 70°C for 1 h.
steps.searchable_summary: methylate alcohol fraction with pyridine and BSTFA

steps.description: All fractions were quantified using a TRACE GC Ultra gas chromatograph equipped with a flame ionization detector.
Individual lipid compounds were identified by comparing their retention times to those of laboratory standard mixtures, or by gas chromatograph mass spectrometry.

Compound-specific stable carbon isotope ratios of individual compounds were determined using a gas chromatograph with split/splitless injector, connected on-line via a GC-Isolink combustion interface to a ConFlo IV and Delta V Advantage isotope ratio mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: determine stable carbon isotope ratios using GC-IRMS

methods.method_name: DNA Extraction, PCR Amplification, Illumina Sequencing, and Data Analysis
methods.description: Method for extracting DNA from sediment samples, amplifying it using PCR, sequencing with Illumina, and analyzing the data.

steps.description: DNA was extracted from both samples of Lake Sempach core sediments and from the slurry sediments at the end of incubations using a FastDNA SPIN Kit for Soil.
steps.searchable_summary: extract DNA using FastDNA SPIN Kit

steps.description: A two-step PCR approach was applied in order to prepare the library for sequencing.
steps.searchable_summary: perform two-step PCR for library preparation

steps.description: Purified indexed amplicons were finally pooled at equimolar concentration into one library, and sequenced on an Illumina MiSeq platform using the 2 × 300 bp paired-end protocol.
steps.searchable_summary: sequence DNA library using Illumina MiSeq

steps.description: Quality of the raw reads was checked using FastQC.
Paired-end read merger was used to merge forward and reverse reads into amplicons of about 419 bp length.
Quality filtering was carried out using PRINSEQ.
Amplicon sequence variants were determined by denoising using the UNOISE algorithm.
Taxonomic assignment was done using SINTAX and the SILVA 16S rRNA reference database.
steps.searchable_summary: assign taxonomy using SINTAX and SILVA database

steps.description: Subsequent data analyses were carried out with Phyloseq in the R environment.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze data using Phyloseq in R
